title: betula pendula var. tristis
intro: Betula pendula var. tristis, also known as the weeping birch, is a deciduous tree native to Europe, known for its slender white trunk and weeping branches. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, including salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of weeping birch include reducing fever, relieving headaches and arthritis, and supporting digestive health. Weeping birch can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory issues.
